question: What were Johnson & Johnson's total sales to customers for fiscal year 2025 (fiscal year ended December 28, 2025)?
answer_notes: Consolidated statements of earnings: Sales to customers, FY2025 primary column; JNJ labels its top-line revenue 'Sales to customers' rather than 'Revenue' or 'Net revenue'

question: What were Johnson & Johnson's net earnings for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated statements of earnings: Net earnings, FY2025; entirely from continuing operations — no discontinued operations in FY2025

question: What was Johnson & Johnson's diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated statements of earnings: Total net earnings per share - diluted; basic EPS was $11.13; average diluted shares outstanding 2,429.4M

question: What were Johnson & Johnson's net earnings for fiscal year 2024, as reported in the comparative column of the FY2025 10-K?
answer_notes: Income statement FY2024 comparative column; significantly lower than FY2025 ($26,804M) primarily due to large litigation charges and acquisition-related costs recognized in FY2024

question: What was Johnson & Johnson's gross profit for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated statements of earnings: Gross profit (Sales to customers $94,193M minus Cost of products sold $30,256M); gross margin approximately 67.9%

question: What was Johnson & Johnson's research and development expense for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated statements of earnings: Research and development expense line; an additional $81M in-process R&D impairments was recorded separately; FY2024 R&D was $17,232M

question: What were Johnson & Johnson's total assets as of December 28, 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated balance sheet: Total assets, Dec 28, 2025; prior year-end (Dec 29, 2024) was $180,104M; increase driven by acquisitions including $17,541M in net acquisition spend in FY2025

question: What was Johnson & Johnson's cash and cash equivalents balance at the end of fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated balance sheet: Cash and cash equivalents, Dec 28, 2025; confirmed by cash flow statement end-of-year balance; prior year was $24,105M

question: What was Johnson & Johnson's net cash provided by operating activities for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated statements of cash flows: Net cash flows from operating activities, FY2025; FY2024 was $24,266M — relatively stable year over year

question: How much did Johnson & Johnson pay in cash dividends per share during fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated statements of equity: Cash dividends paid per share, FY2025; FY2024 was $4.91; FY2023 was $4.70 — consistent annual dividend growth across three years

question: What was Johnson & Johnson's net profit margin (net earnings as a percentage of total sales to customers) for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Requires dividing net earnings by sales to customers from the income statement; acceptable range: 28.2%–28.8%

question: Did Johnson & Johnson's net earnings nearly double from fiscal year 2024 to fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Net earnings grew from $14,066M (FY2024) to $26,804M (FY2025), an increase of $12,738M (+90.6%); requires reading both columns of the comparative income statement in the 10-K

question: By what percentage did Johnson & Johnson's annual sales to customers grow from fiscal year 2024 to fiscal year 2025?
answer: Approximately 6.0% (from $88,821M in FY2024 to $94,193M in FY2025, an increase of $5,372M)
answer_notes: Requires reading both FY2025 and FY2024 columns of the income statement; 5372/88821=6.05%; acceptable range: 5.8%–6.2%

question: How many total securities does Johnson & Johnson have listed on the New York Stock Exchange as disclosed in its FY2025 10-K cover page?
answer: 11 — common stock (JNJ) plus ten note series: JNJ28, JNJ29B, JNJ32, JNJ33B, JNJ35, JNJ36A, JNJ37B, JNJ44, JNJ45, and JNJ55
answer_notes: Entity property: securities registered under Section 12(b) from 10-K cover page; JNJ has the most NYSE-listed securities of any company in the corpus (XOM has 4, WMT has 5 bond series + common)

question: What was Exxon Mobil Corporation's net income attributable to ExxonMobil for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated Statement of Income: Net income attributable to ExxonMobil, FY2025; FY2024 was $33,680M; FY2023 was $36,010M — three-year declining trend

question: What was Exxon Mobil Corporation's diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated Statement of Income: EPS diluted, FY2025 annual

question: What were Exxon Mobil Corporation's total revenues for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated Statement of Income: Total revenues, FY2025 annual

question: What were Exxon Mobil Corporation's total assets as of December 31, 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated Balance Sheet: Total Assets, Dec 31, 2025; prior year-end Dec 31, 2024 was $453,475M — slight year-over-year decrease

question: What was Exxon Mobil Corporation's capital expenditure for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated Statement of Cash Flows: capital expenditures, FY2025 annual

question: What was Exxon Mobil Corporation's net cash provided by operating activities for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Consolidated Statement of Cash Flows: operating cash flow, FY2025 annual

question: Who is Exxon Mobil Corporation's independent registered public accounting firm as disclosed in the FY2025 10-K?
answer_notes: PwC has served as ExxonMobil's auditor since 1934; same firm as Johnson & Johnson (confirmed via PCAOB ID 238 in jnj-10k.pdf)

question: Who is the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Exxon Mobil Corporation as disclosed in the FY2025 10-K?
answer_notes: Signing officer of the 10-K annual report; CFO is Neil A. Hansen

question: Did Exxon Mobil Corporation's net income increase or decrease from fiscal year 2024 to fiscal year 2025, and by approximately how much?
answer: Decreased by $4,836 million (from $33,680M in FY2024 to $28,844M in FY2025, a decline of approximately 14.4%)
answer_notes: Requires reading both FY2024 and FY2025 columns of the comparative income statement; three-year declining trend: $36,010M (FY2023) → $33,680M (FY2024) → $28,844M (FY2025)

question: What was Exxon Mobil Corporation's approximate net income margin (net income as a percentage of total revenues) for fiscal year 2025?
answer_notes: Requires dividing net income attributable to ExxonMobil by total revenues; acceptable range: 8.5%–8.9%

question: What was JPMorgan Chase's net income for Q3 2025 (quarter ended September 30, 2025)?
answer_notes: Financial highlights table: Net income, 3Q25 column; Q3 2024 net income was $12,898M

question: What was JPMorgan Chase's total net revenue for Q3 2025 (quarter ended September 30, 2025)?
answer_notes: Financial highlights table: Total net revenue, 3Q25 column; Q3 2024 was $42,654M

question: What was JPMorgan Chase's diluted earnings per share for Q3 2025?
answer_notes: Financial highlights table: Net income Diluted EPS, 3Q25 column; basic EPS was $5.08

question: What was JPMorgan Chase's net income for the nine months ended September 30, 2025?
answer_notes: Financial highlights table: Net income, Nine months ended Sep 30, 2025; nine-month 2024 was $44,466M (slightly higher due to one-time Visa share gain in 2024)

question: What were JPMorgan Chase's total assets as of September 30, 2025?
answer_notes: Financial highlights table: Total assets (period-end), Sep 30, 2025; notably higher than the FY2025 year-end figure of $4,424,900M from the 10-K — assets contracted in Q4 2025

question: What was JPMorgan Chase's book value per share as of September 30, 2025?
answer_notes: Financial highlights table: Book value per share, Sep 30, 2025; tangible book value per share (TBVPS) was $105.70

question: What was JPMorgan Chase's tangible book value per share (TBVPS) as of September 30, 2025?
answer_notes: Financial highlights table: Tangible book value per share (TBVPS), Sep 30, 2025; book value per share was $124.96; the $19.26 difference reflects goodwill and intangible assets excluded from tangible book value

question: How many common shares of JPMorgan Chase & Co. were outstanding as of September 30, 2025?
answer_notes: Cover page: Number of shares of common stock outstanding as of September 30, 2025; financial highlights confirms 2,722.2M shares at period-end

question: Did JPMorgan Chase's net income for Q3 2025 exceed Q3 2024 net income, and by approximately what percentage?
answer: Yes, increased by approximately 11.6% (from $12,898M in Q3 2024 to $14,393M in Q3 2025, a gain of $1,495M)
answer_notes: Requires reading both Q3 2025 and Q3 2024 columns from the financial highlights table in the same 10-Q

question: Were JPMorgan Chase's total assets higher at September 30, 2025 (from the 10-Q) than at December 31, 2025 (from the 10-K)?
answer: Yes — total assets were $4,560,205M at September 30, 2025 versus $4,424,900M at December 31, 2025, meaning assets contracted by approximately $135,305M during Q4 2025
answer_notes: Cross-document comparison between jpm-10q.pdf (Sep 30 balance sheet) and jpm-10k.html (Dec 31 balance sheet); the Q3 peak exceeding year-end is a non-obvious result that tests whether the system reads the correct period from each file

question: How did Johnson & Johnson's total assets change between the end of Q3 2025 (September 28, 2025) and the end of fiscal year 2025 (December 28, 2025)?
answer: Increased from $192,816 million (Q3 end, from jnj-10q.html) to $199,210 million (FY2025 year-end, from jnj-10k.pdf), an increase of approximately $6,394 million
answer_notes: Requires combining the Q3 balance sheet from the HTML 10-Q with the year-end balance sheet from the PDF 10-K; tests cross-format retrieval

question: Which companies in the corpus share the same independent registered public accounting firm, based on their annual 10-K filings?
answer: Johnson & Johnson, Exxon Mobil, and JPMorgan Chase all use PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP; Apple and Walmart both use Ernst & Young LLP
answer_notes: Requires traversing auditor entity across all five 10-K filings: JNJ (PCAOB ID 238 = PwC), XOM (PwC since 1934), JPM (PwC, PCAOB ID 238, confirmed in jpm-10k.html Item 8); Apple (EY since 2009); Walmart (EY since 1969)

question: Between Johnson & Johnson and Exxon Mobil Corporation, which company reported higher net income for fiscal year 2025 as disclosed in their respective PDF 10-K filings?
answer: Exxon Mobil Corporation ($28,844 million vs. Johnson & Johnson's $26,804 million)
answer_notes: Requires reading net income from both PDF 10-K filings; both fiscal years end in calendar Q4 2025 (XOM: Dec 31, JNJ: Dec 28); XOM leads by approximately $2,040M

question: Which companies in the corpus have their annual 10-K filings stored in PDF format rather than HTML?
answer: Johnson & Johnson (jnj-10k.pdf) and Exxon Mobil Corporation (xom-10k.pdf)
answer_notes: Graph-level format property: Apple (aapl-10k.htm), Walmart (wmt-10k.html), and JPMorgan (jpm-10k.html) have HTML 10-Ks; only JNJ and XOM are PDF

question: Between Johnson & Johnson and Exxon Mobil Corporation, which company has more securities listed on the NYSE as disclosed in their respective 10-K cover pages, and how many does each have?
answer: Johnson & Johnson has more — 11 securities (common stock plus 10 note series: JNJ28 through JNJ55) versus ExxonMobil's 4 (XOM common stock plus XOM28, XOM32, XOM39A notes)
answer_notes: Entity property: securities registered under Section 12(b); requires counting from both PDF 10-K cover pages

question: Which companies in the corpus have their quarterly 10-Q filing in PDF format, and are both equally usable for financial content retrieval?
answer: JPMorgan Chase (jpm-10q.pdf, 270-page full filing) and Exxon Mobil (xom-10q.pdf); however, xom-10q.pdf is an EDGAR inline viewer cover page only — not a full 10-Q document and contains no financial statement data
answer_notes: Graph-level format property with an asymmetry caveat; jpm-10q.pdf is a complete 270-page 10-Q with full financial statements; xom-10q.pdf is a single-page inline viewer screenshot — a hallucination risk if the system attempts to extract XOM quarterly financials from it
